query: add a `loading` boolean prop. When loading is true, the button must be disabled (its `disabled` HTML attribute true) AND render the text 'Loading…' INSTEAD OF children. When loading is false or undefined, behavior is unchanged.
check: node --input-type=module -e "import {readFileSync} from 'node:fs';import a from 'node:assert/strict';const s=readFileSync(process.argv[1],'utf8');a.ok(/\bloading\b/.test(s),'loading prop missing');a.ok(s.includes('Loading…')||s.includes('Loading...'),'Loading text missing');a.ok(/disabled=\{[^}]*loading|loading\s*\|\|\s*disabled|disabled\s*\|\|\s*loading/.test(s),'disabled not wired to loading');" $FILE

query: wrap the exported Button in React.memo — the module must still expose a `Button` named export (not default) that is a memoized component.
check: node --input-type=module -e "import {readFileSync} from 'node:fs';import a from 'node:assert/strict';const s=readFileSync(process.argv[1],'utf8');a.ok(/React\.memo|\bmemo\s*\(/.test(s),'memo not used');a.ok(/export\s+(const|default)\s+Button|export\s*\{\s*Button/.test(s),'Button export missing');" $FILE
